role: Iranian arms dealer; key intermediary in the Iran-Contra affair
bio: Manucher Ghorbanifar is an expatriate Iranian arms dealer who served as the principal intermediary in the [Iran-Contra affair](/events/iran-contra-affair-1985). A former [SAVAK](/orgs/savak) associate under the Shah, he built a fortune in shipping before the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979) and relocated to France after his assets were seized. In 1985, Ghorbanifar proposed the arms-for-hostages arrangement to U.S. and Israeli officials, brokering the initial shipments of TOW missiles and Hawk missile parts to Iran during the [Iran-Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war). He added a 41-percent markup to arms prices and was described by American officials as playing both sides for commercial advantage. The CIA issued a 'burn notice' on Ghorbanifar in 1984 after he failed multiple polygraph tests, labeling him an unreliable source. He re-emerged as a back-channel intelligence source to Pentagon officials during the lead-up to the 2003 Iraq War.
editorial_notes: Born c. 1945. Former SAVAK associate. CIA burn notice 1984. Key intermediary 1985–1986. Added 41% markup. Resurfaced c. 2003 with Pentagon contacts re: Iran regime change.
